questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A light source, emitting three wavelengths $5000 Å, 6000 Å$ and $7000 Å$, has a total power of $10^{-3} \mathrm{~W}$ and a beam diameter $2 \times 10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}$. The power density is distributed equally amongst the three wavelengths. The beam shines normally on a metallic surface of area on $10^{-4} \mathrm{~m}^2$ and having a work function of 1.9 eV . Assume that each photon liberates an electron, calculate the charge emitted per unit area in one second(mark the answer in C/m²-sec)
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \phi=1.9 \mathrm{eV} \\
& \lambda_{\mathrm{th}}=6526 Å
\end{aligned}$

Only $5000 Å$ and $6000 Å$ will liberate electrons $h$, no of photons, $n=\frac{10^{-3} \lambda_1}{3 h c}+\frac{10^{-3} \lambda_2}{3 h c}$
$\text { Charge emitted/Area }=\left[\frac{4 n e}{\pi d^2}\right]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A gas containing hydrogen like ions with atomic no Z , emits photons in transition $\mathrm{n}+2 \rightarrow \mathrm{n}$, where $\mathrm{n}=\mathrm{Z}$. These photons fall on a metallic plate and eject electrons having minimum de-Broglie wavelength $\lambda$ of $5 \mathrm{~A}^{\circ}$. Find the value of ' $Z^{\prime}$ if the work function of metal is 4.2 eV .
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
E= & -(13.6 \mathrm{eV}) Z^2\left(\frac{1}{(Z+2)^2}-\frac{1}{Z^2}\right) \\
& =-13.6 \times\left(\frac{Z^2-(Z+2)^2}{(Z+2)^2}\right)=\frac{4(Z+1) \times 13.6}{(Z+2)^2} \mathrm{eV}
\end{aligned}$...(1)

Now energy of electron is $k=\frac{h^2}{2 \lambda^2 m}$; (we have $\left.\lambda=\frac{h}{\sqrt{2 m k}}\right)$ or, $k=6 \mathrm{eV}$
So, $\frac{4(Z+1) \times 13.6}{(Z+2)^2}=6+4.2=10.2 \mathrm{eV}$
Or, $\frac{(Z+1)}{(Z+2)^2}=\frac{3}{16} \Rightarrow(Z-2)(3 Z+2)=0$
So, the value of $Z=2$ (neglecting the negative/fractional value)
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: An electron and a proton are separated by a large distance and the electron approaches the proton with a kinetic energy of 2 eV . If the electron is captured by the proton to form a hydrogen atom in the ground state, what wavelength (in $Å$ ) photon would be given off?
questions.solution:
Energy of hydrogen atom $=-13.6 \mathrm{ev}$
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{h c}{\lambda}=13.6+2 \\ & \lambda \approx 796 Å\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Light from a discharge tube containing hydrogen atoms falls on a piece of sodium due to the transition of electron from $4^{\text {th }}$ orbit to $2^{\text {nd }}$ orbit. Work function of sodium is 1.83 eV . The fastest moving photoelectron is allowed to enter in a magnetic field, which is perpendicular to the direction of motion of photoelectron as shown in figure. Find distance (in $\mu \mathrm{m}$ ) covered by the electron in the magnetic field. $\left[\mathrm{B}=1 \mathrm{Tesla}, \pi^2=10\right.$, mass of electron $\left.=9 \times 10^{-31} \mathrm{Kg}\right]$ where R is the radius of the path that the most energetic electron takes in the presence of applied magnetic field.
questions.solution:
Energy of incident photon $=13.6\left[\frac{1}{2^2}-\frac{1}{4^2}\right]=13.6\left[\frac{1}{4}-\frac{1}{16}\right]=13.6 \times \frac{3}{16}=2.55 \mathrm{eV}$
Energy of photon $=$ work function $(W)+(\text { K.E. })_{\max }$
Deflection $180^{\circ}-60^{\circ}=120^{\circ}$ (from geometry) Radius $\frac{m v^2}{R}=\mathrm{qvB} \Rightarrow \mathrm{R}=\mathrm{mv} / \mathrm{qB}$
circumference of part inside the magnetic field
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
